sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CH1A.DE C.H. Robinson XETRA -22.62% to €130.00 on 16 Feb 2026 after hours: risk ahead
feed.summary: Shares of C.H. Robinson Worldwide Inc (CH1A.DE) on XETRA experienced a significant after-hours drop of 22.62% to €130.00 on February 16, 2026, following an intraday sell-off and thin trading volume. Despite solid fundamentals and a "BUY" rating from Meyka AI with projected upside, the stock faces risks from freight-rate volatility and upcoming earnings. Investors are advised to monitor volume, receivables, and the April 29, 2026, earnings release for future direction.

feed.title: AI Spending Shock Triggers Big Tech Selloff And Small-Cap Revival
feed.summary: The market is experiencing a reversal from big tech leadership due to investor concerns over surging AI capital expenditures impacting future margins. This shift is leading to a selloff in top tech stocks and a rotation of capital into smaller companies, particularly those supplying the AI build-out, which are seen as having potential for a rebound and structural growth. JPMorgan identifies "AI-resilient" large-cap software companies as opportunities, while Franklin Templeton highlights small-cap firms benefiting from AI's productivity gains and infrastructure needs.

feed.title: Smithfield Foods to Build New Facility in South Dakota
feed.summary: Smithfield Foods plans to construct a new, highly automated packaged meats and fresh pork processing facility in Sioux Falls, South Dakota, with an estimated investment of up to $1.3 billion. This new facility will replace its century-old existing plant, aiming to boost efficiency and support the regional agricultural economy. Construction is projected to start in spring 2026 with production commencing by the end of 2028, pending regulatory and board approvals.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Company Stock Outlook: Q4 Miss Pressures Shares, Yet Analysts Still See 30% Upside Scenario
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company reported a Q4 2025 net sales miss and issued fiscal 2026 guidance below analyst expectations, primarily due to shifts in consumer spending and the comparative impact of storm recovery efforts from the previous year. Despite these pressures, analysts project a 30% upside scenario for the stock, believing that strategic initiatives like new store openings and Direct Sales, alongside a rebound in discretionary spending, could drive future growth. The article presents a valuation model suggesting a "Hold" rating due to annualized returns slightly below the typical equity hurdle, pending more established execution of these growth strategies.

feed.title: 10 Information Technology Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies ten Information Technology stocks that are exhibiting "whale alerts" based on unusual options activity. It details specific call and put option trades for companies like NVIDIA, Intel, Apple, and AMD, including strike prices, expiration dates, sentiment, and total trade values. The piece explains how these whale alerts can signal potential trading opportunities for investors.

feed.title: Hormel Foods Names Will Bonifant Group Vice President and Chief Supply Chain Officer
feed.summary: Hormel Foods Corporation has appointed Will Bonifant as Group Vice President and Chief Supply Chain Officer, effective March 9. Bonifant, previously with The Hershey Company, will oversee Hormel's global supply chain operations including procurement, manufacturing, and logistics. His experience in scaling complex operations and driving transformation is expected to strengthen Hormel's supply chain strategy.

feed.title: Billionaire BlackRock CEO Larry Fink Says 'Almost No One Is Close' To Saving The Nearly $2.1M Americans Claim They Need For A Comfortable Retirement
feed.summary: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink highlighted a significant retirement savings gap in the U.S., noting that Americans believe they need over $2 million for a comfortable retirement, yet "almost no one is close." He attributes this shortfall partly to the different investment vehicles used by pensions versus 401(k)s, with pensions often including higher-performing private assets. Fink emphasizes that incorporating private assets could significantly boost 401(k) returns and extend retirement funding, despite growing anxiety about the economy.

feed.title: Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) Sees Significant Decline in Short Interest
feed.summary: Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) recently experienced a significant 16.7% decline in short interest as of January 30th, indicating reduced bearish pressure on the stock. This comes after the chipmaker beat quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, though revenue was down year-over-year and negative margins persist. Wall Street sentiment remains mixed, with analysts weighing potential AI demand tailwinds against ongoing competitive risks and execution challenges for the company.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals Stock (APD): Q1 2026 Earnings Beat, Analyst Outlook | 2026 Review - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals (APD) stock has risen 13.3% year-to-date, outperforming the S&P 500, despite an 11.2% decrease over the past 52 weeks. The company reported strong fiscal first-quarter 2026 results, with adjusted EPS increasing 10% to $3.16 and revenue growing 6% to $3.1 billion, both exceeding analyst expectations. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a mean price target implying a 7.3% premium.

feed.title: Before Henry Ford Changed the World, He Had to Start Over – Twice
feed.summary: Henry Ford's journey to revolutionizing the automotive industry wasn't straightforward. He had to overcome significant setbacks and restart his ventures multiple times before achieving lasting success with the Ford Motor Company. This demonstrates his persistence and innovative spirit in the face of early failures.

feed.title: What to Expect Ahead of Cadence Design's Q4 Earnings Release?
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) is set to release its Q4 2025 earnings on February 17, with analysts predicting a slight increase in earnings per share and revenue. Despite macroeconomic uncertainties and competition, the company's strong focus on AI-driven solutions for semiconductor and system design, along with its ratable software model and strategic acquisitions, are expected to be key drivers. However, Zacks' model does not predict an earnings beat for Cadence this quarter.

feed.title: Awning collapses at O’Reilly Auto Parts in Maple Heights
feed.summary: An awning collapsed at the O'Reilly Auto Parts store in Maple Heights, Ohio, within the Southgate Shopping Center on Sunday afternoon. No injuries were reported in the incident. Firefighters responded to the scene, and the store was closed for the day while the building department works to stabilize the awning and arrange for safe repairs.

feed.title: Lululemon Stock Analysis 2026: Performance, Price Target & Outlook - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Lululemon's stock has seen a significant decline over the past year, underperforming both the S&P 500 and the Amplify Online Retail ETF, despite exceeding Q3 earnings expectations. Analysts currently rate the stock as a "Hold," with a consensus price target suggesting a modest upside from current levels. The report details the company's financial performance, analyst recommendations, and projected outlook.

feed.title: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From BD's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) reported strong Q4 results with revenue and EPS exceeding analyst estimates, driven by growth in biologic drug delivery, advanced tissue regeneration, and pharmacy automation. Despite challenges from China and vaccine-related pressures, the company maintained focus on investments in growth platforms and productivity. Analyst questions highlighted concerns about future guidance, portfolio changes, and the long-term outlook for GLP-1 devices, with management providing clarifying responses.

feed.title: AGI Gears Up to Report Q4 Earnings: What's in Store for the Stock?
feed.summary: Alamos Gold (AGI) is set to release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on February 18, with the Zacks Consensus Estimate at 49 cents per share, indicating a 96% year-over-year growth despite a recent downward revision. The company's Q4 production reached 141,500 ounces, and record revenues of $568 million were achieved from gold sales, although the average realized price was impacted by a prepayment facility. Despite a strong performance forecast, the Zacks Model does not conclusively predict an earnings beat due to a 0.00% Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank #3.

feed.title: Balanced View on Eversource Energy: Discounted Valuation Offset by Near-Term Earnings and Regulatory Risks Supporting a Hold Rating
feed.summary: UBS analyst William Appicelli has maintained a Hold rating on Eversource Energy (ES) stock, citing a balanced view. While the stock trades at a discount to peers on 2028 earnings, near-term earnings growth until 2028 is expected to be below the company's 5-7% target, and regulatory decisions in Connecticut pose uncertainties. Appicelli increased his price target reflecting refined EPS estimates and a higher sector valuation, concluding the shares are reasonably valued given these factors.

feed.title: Energy Stocks Are Printing Cash — So Why Are They Still Cheap?
feed.summary: Energy companies are generating impressive cash flows and offer high free cash flow yields, yet their valuations remain low compared to the broader market, reflecting persistent pessimism. Despite strong fundamentals, institutional ownership is below historical norms, influenced by past divestment trends and tech sector outperformance. However, global energy demand is projected to continue rising, suggesting the sector may be undervalued and due for a repricing.

feed.title: JPMorgan Unit Must Defend Lawsuit Over ‘Cash Sweep’ Program
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is required to defend against a proposed class action lawsuit concerning its "Cash Sweep" program. The lawsuit alleges that the firm steered uninvested customer cash into low-interest bank deposit accounts, paying between 0.01% and 0.03% interest, while prevailing market rates were significantly higher, up to 5.33%. Customers claim this discrepancy allowed JPMorgan and its affiliates to profit unfairly.

feed.title: George Soros Reduces Stake in Smurfit WestRock PLC by 68.99%
feed.summary: Billionaire investor George Soros significantly reduced his stake in Smurfit WestRock PLC by 68.99% during the fourth quarter of 2025, according to a recent 13F filing. His investment firm, Soros Fund Management LLC, made substantial new buys in Dayforce Inc, Exact Sciences Corp, and New Gold Inc, while also increasing positions in Microsoft Corp and Electronic Arts Inc. The portfolio overview highlights top holdings in Amazon.com Inc, Alphabet Inc, and Salesforce Inc, with a concentrated focus across technology, financial services, and consumer cyclical industries.

feed.title: Abbott class action claims glucose sensors sold with defect
feed.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Abbott Diabetes Care Inc. and Abbott Laboratories, alleging that their FreeStyle Libre 3 and FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus glucose sensors are defective. Plaintiff Bijoy Shroff claims these sensors provide falsely low glucose readings and that Abbott knowingly sold them without disclosing this defect, only initiating a recall after numerous injuries and deaths were reported. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers, accusing Abbott of fraudulent omission, unjust enrichment, and violating consumer protection laws.

feed.title: A Look Ahead: National Energy Services's Earnings Forecast
feed.summary: National Energy Services (NASDAQ:NESR) is poised to release its quarterly earnings on February 17, 2026, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $0.26. Investors are keen to see if the company surpasses expectations and provides strong guidance, which historically impacts stock prices. The company has a positive track record, having beaten EPS estimates in the previous quarter, and its shares have seen a significant 120.42% increase over the last 52 weeks.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges PomDoctor, Ltd. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against PomDoctor, Ltd. (NASDAQ: POM) and its officers, alleging federal securities law violations. The lawsuit claims that PomDoctor engaged in a fraudulent stock promotion scheme involving social media misinformation and coordinated share dumping by insiders, misleading investors between October 9, 2025, and December 11, 2025. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 6, 2026.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Oracle Corporation
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Oracle Corporation (ORCL) and its officers, alleging violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims that Oracle misrepresented its AI infrastructure strategy, leading to increased capital expenditures without equivalent revenue growth, and that increased spending created risks for the company's debt, credit rating, and free cash flow. Investors who purchased Oracle securities between June 12, 2025, and December 16, 2025, are encouraged to join the case, with an April 6, 2026, deadline to request to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges uniQure N.V. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against uniQure N.V. (NASDAQ: QURE) and its officers, alleging violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims that uniQure made false and misleading statements regarding the FDA approval status of its Pivotal Study design and the likelihood of delaying its BLA timeline. Investors who purchased uniQure securities between September 24, 2025, and October 31, 2025, are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 13, 2026.

feed.title: Apple Announces Special Event In March As New iPhone And Mac Rumors Swirl
feed.summary: Apple has announced a "Special Experience" event in March 2026 across New York City, London, and Shanghai, sparking speculation about new product launches. While the event is expected to be a hands-on session for media, rumors suggest that new iPhones, Macs, and iPads could be unveiled prior to or around this date. Specifically, the iPhone 17e is anticipated to be a highlight, potentially featuring a Dynamic Island, a more powerful A19 chip, and MagSafe capabilities.

feed.title: Home Depot's strict checkout rule comes into question as shopper notices 'cages'
feed.summary: Home Depot's anti-theft policy of activating power tools at checkout, announced in 2021, has not been fully implemented, leading shoppers to notice tools still locked in "cages." The method, similar to gift card activation, was intended to combat rising retail theft by rendering stolen tools inoperable. Lowe's announced a similar initiative in 2023 but has also not yet implemented it.

feed.title: Host Hotels Gears Up to Report Q4 Earnings: Key Factors to Consider
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) is poised to release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on February 18, with expectations of revenue and adjusted funds from operations (AFFO) per share growth. The company's strategic portfolio of luxury hotels in top U.S. markets and the Sunbelt region, along with improvements in group and transient demand, are key factors expected to contribute positively to its performance. Despite a projected decrease in occupancy rate for Q4 2025 and an anticipated rise in interest expenses, Host Hotels is predicted to report an AFFO per share surprise due to positive Earnings ESP and its Zacks Rank.

feed.title: Distribution Management Named HP Inc. 2025 Partner of the Year
feed.summary: HP Inc. recognized Distribution Management as its 2025 Partner of the Year for outstanding performance, innovative solutions, and strategic growth. This marks the second consecutive year Distribution Management has received this honor, highlighting their significant contributions as the U.S. Print Hardware Distributor of the Year within the HP ecosystem. The award acknowledges their commitment to delivering value and excellence to customers through strong collaboration with HP.

feed.title: Merchants Bancorp Index Debut And Buyback Put Valuation In Focus
feed.summary: Merchants Bancorp (NasdaqCM:MBIN) has been included in major S&P indices and authorized a significant stock repurchase program, shifting its capital allocation priorities. The company reports improved asset quality and record tangible book value, contrasting with recent insider selling. These developments highlight the company's increased visibility, potential impact on share count, and a mix of positive financial metrics with insider activity that raises questions about current valuation.

feed.title: (RGCO) Volatility Zones as Tactical Triggers
feed.summary: This article analyzes Rgc Resources Inc. (NASDAQ: RGCO) using AI models to identify volatility zones and tactical triggers for trading. It highlights a weak near-term sentiment which could lead to bearish positioning and outlines three trading strategies: Position Trading, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging, tailored for different risk profiles. The analysis also provides multi-timeframe signal support and resistance levels.

feed.title: Fincantieri secures order from Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings for three new cruise ships
feed.summary: Fincantieri has secured a significant order from Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings for the construction of three new next-generation cruise ships. These vessels, scheduled for delivery between 2036 and 2037, will be built for Norwegian Cruise Line, Oceania Cruises, and Regent Seven Seas Cruises, reinforcing Fincantieri's long-standing partnership and leadership in the cruise sector. The order highlights the commitment of both companies to technological innovation, comfort, and environmental sustainability in shipbuilding.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies awarded nearly $400 million contract to supply propulsion for THAAD missile defense interceptors
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies has secured a contract worth almost $400 million to provide propulsion systems for the Missile Defense Agency's Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) system. This contract encompasses the production of solid rocket boost motors and Liquid Divert and Attitude Control Systems for the critical U.S. missile defense system. THAAD is renowned for its 100% success rate in intercept tests, capable of intercepting targets both inside and outside the atmosphere.

feed.title: Fairlife, Coca-Cola Class Action Over Alleged Cow Abuse Trimmed
feed.summary: A federal judge has narrowed a class action lawsuit against Coca-Cola Co. and its dairy unit fairlife LLC, which claimed the companies misled consumers about animal welfare. The judge preserved certain claims that argue the company's cartoon-cow logo and "fairlife" name imply ethical treatment of cows, despite alleged abuse at its facilities. These remaining claims involve breach of express warranty and a violation of California’s false-advertising law.

feed.title: Builders FirstSource earnings up next: Can margins weather downturn? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Builders FirstSource (BLDR) is set to report fourth-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating a decline in revenue and EPS amid challenging housing market conditions. Investors will focus on comments regarding commodity prices, gross margin trajectory, and the 2026 outlook, particularly EBITDA guidance, as building material costs increase. Despite a history of beating expectations due to its scale and integration, the stock is trading below its 52-week high, indicating investor demand for clarity on margin stability and volume recovery.

feed.title: Fairlife, Coca-Cola Class Action Over Alleged Cow Abuse Trimmed
feed.summary: A federal judge partially dismissed several claims in a class action lawsuit against Fairlife and Coca-Cola Co. The lawsuit alleged the companies misled consumers about animal welfare at their facilities, implying fair treatment through their brand name and logo. While some claims were trimmed, those based on the cartoon-cow logo and Fairlife name, including breach of express warranty and violation of California's false-advertising law, remain active.

feed.title: Allegion earnings on deck as security maker tests margin shift By Investing.com
feed.summary: Allegion (ALLE) is set to report fourth-quarter results, with investors focusing on the company's shift towards higher-margin electronic security products to counter expected revenue declines and international market pressures. Analysts anticipate $1.98 EPS and $1.04 billion in revenue. Key areas of investor attention include the contribution of electronic offerings to growth, performance differentiation between Americas and International segments, and the residential-versus-commercial dynamic in the access control market.

feed.title: Virginie Helias: How P&G Champions Healthcare Sustainability
feed.summary: Virginie Helias, Procter & Gamble's Chief Sustainability Officer, has been ranked sixth in Sustainability Magazine's Top 250 Sustainability Leaders 2026 for her dedication to integrating sustainability. She focuses on minimizing environmental impact and supporting communities through health and hygiene initiatives, seeing sustainability as a driver for innovation and brand building. Helias will also be speaking at Sustainability LIVE: The Net Zero Summit to discuss the evolving role of a Chief Sustainability Officer.

feed.title: KWHP and Ascendant Joint Venture Acquires Sotherly
feed.summary: Kemmons Wilson Hospitality Partners (KWHP) and Ascendant Capital Partners (Ascendant) have successfully completed their acquisition of Sotherly Hotels Inc. through their joint venture, KW Kingfisher LLC. The deal, valued at $2.25 per share in cash for all outstanding Sotherly common stock, expands KWHP's and Ascendant's hospitality portfolios into strategic Southeastern U.S. markets. Schulte Hospitality Group will take over operations for Sotherly's 10 full-service hotels and its condo-hotel management agreement.

feed.title: JP Morgan Raises Target Price on Truist Financial (TFC) to $47, Keeps Neutral Call
feed.summary: JPMorgan raised its target price on Truist Financial (TFC) to $57.00 from $51.50, maintaining a Neutral rating, following the bank's Q4 2025 earnings release. The adjustment reflects JPMorgan's updated forecasts for large-cap banks, driven by favorable economic trends, steady fundamentals, sticky inflation, a positive regulatory environment, and increased M&A activity. Truist reported a 5.7% YoY increase in net income and a 9.9% YoY rise in diluted earnings per share for Q4 2025, primarily due to a 3.1% increase in net interest income.

feed.title: Allegion earnings on deck as security maker tests margin shift
feed.summary: Allegion PLC is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, with investors focusing on whether its shift to higher-margin electronic security products can offset potential revenue declines and international market pressures. Analysts predict adjusted earnings of $1.98 per share and revenue of $1.04 billion, and the stock currently has a cautious analyst sentiment with mostly "hold" ratings. The report will also reveal performance differences between its Americas and International segments, and how the company is balancing residential market weakness with commercial strength.

feed.title: JP Morgan Raises Target Price on Citi (C), Keeps Overweight Call
feed.summary: JPMorgan raised its target price on Citigroup (C) to $134 from $130, reiterating an Overweight rating, following an update to large-cap bank forecasts after Q4 2025 results. The firm favors bank stocks due to good economic trends, steady fundamentals, sticky inflation, a favorable regulatory environment, and increased M&A activity. Citi's Q4 earnings showed a 25.8% YoY adjusted net income growth, driven by a 14.1% increase in net interest income and growth in its loan book.

feed.title: Invesco International Growth Fund Q4 2025 Portfolio Update
feed.summary: The Invesco International Growth Fund underperformed in industrials, consumer discretionary, and communication services during Q4 2025 due to stock selection, but saw outperformance in financials, healthcare, and energy. The fund initiated five new positions, four of which are disclosed, including Banco Santander, and eliminated seven positions. Key additions like Banco Santander were driven by attractive valuation and strategic focus, while some eliminations, such as Dassault Systemes, were due to concerns over business expansion and market headwinds.

feed.title: Should INVH Stock Be in Your Portfolio Pre-Q4 Earnings?
feed.summary: Invitation Homes (INVH) is expected to report increased revenues and FFO per share for Q4 2025, despite broader softening in the apartment REIT market. The company's diverse single-family rental portfolio in high-growth markets and technological enhancements are likely to contribute positively, though high rental property supply in some areas could be a headwind. Analysts anticipate FFO per share to remain unchanged at 48 cents, with Zacks' quantitative model not conclusively predicting an FFO surprise.

feed.title: JP Morgan Overweight on BNY Mellon (BK) with a Target Price of $128.50
feed.summary: JP Morgan has reiterated its "Overweight" rating on BNY Mellon (BK) and increased its target price to $128.50 from $125.00, following an update to its financial models for large-cap banks after Q4 2025 earnings. BNY Mellon's strong Q4 performance was driven by a 26.3% YoY growth in net income and a significant 5.3% increase in fee revenue, particularly from investment services. The company's management targets 5%+ YoY revenue growth for 2026.

feed.title: Bank of New York Mellon Is Up 16% in the Last 6 Months. Here’s How Much the Stock Could Rise in 2026
feed.summary: Bank of New York Mellon (BNY) stock has risen 16% in the last six months, reaching $118 per share, driven by consistent execution, improved efficiency, and durable fee growth. The company's structural transformation and disciplined expense control have strengthened investor confidence. A valuation model suggests the stock could reach $136 by 2026, implying a 16% upside, due to margin durability, cross-platform integration, and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: JP Morgan Increases Target Price on Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) to $21
feed.summary: JP Morgan has raised its target price on Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) to $21 from $20, maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This adjustment follows the release of Q4 2025 earnings, which showed a 16.6% year-over-year growth in attributable net income. The firm anticipates bank stocks will outperform in 2026 due to positive economic trends, steady fundamentals, sticky inflation, a favorable regulatory environment, and increased bank consolidations.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Raises Price Target on Pinnacle Financial (PNFP) to $116, Keeps Overweight Call
feed.summary: Evercore ISI increased its price target on Pinnacle Financial (PNFP) to $116 from $115, maintaining an Outperform rating after re-evaluating regional banks following Q4 2025 results. Pinnacle Financial reported strong Q4 earnings with a 12.5% YoY rise in net income to $166.0 million, driven by growth in net interest income and earning assets. The company also provided optimistic guidance for 2026, including significant cost synergies from its merger with Synovus and projected asset growth.

feed.title: Leidos earnings in focus amid questions on VA business outlook
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings Inc. is set to report its fourth-quarter results, with investor attention focused on the sustainability of its high-margin Veterans Affairs medical exams business amidst increased competition. Analysts anticipate earnings of $2.60 per share on $4.31 billion in revenue. The company's recent contract wins and acquisition of ENTRUST Solutions Group will also be scrutinized for their potential to offset any challenges in the VA segment.

feed.title: Ryder System, Inc. (NYSE:R) Short Interest Up 29.0% in January
feed.summary: Short interest in Ryder System, Inc. (NYSE:R) increased by 29.0% in January, reaching 902,550 shares, which represents about 2.4% of the stock. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $215.44, and several firms recently raised price targets. Despite slightly missing quarterly EPS and revenue estimates, Ryder announced FY2026 guidance and a quarterly dividend of $0.91 per share.

feed.title: Fincantieri Receives Order for Three Cruise Ships from Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings
feed.summary: Fincantieri has secured a significant order from Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. for three new-generation cruise ships, subject to financing. These vessels will be built for Norwegian Cruise Line, Oceania Cruises, and Regent Seven Seas Cruises, with deliveries scheduled between 2036 and 2037 from Fincantieri's Italian shipyards. The ships will feature technological innovation, comfort, and environmental sustainability, with varying gross tonnages and berth capacities for each brand.
